title: Document merge-retry cleanup and operator follow-up
description: Capture the operator-facing cleanup note required after the fix lands, including deletion of the stranded merge-conflict bead and retry of the blocked feature merge, or explicitly record that no persistent repo docs need updating beyond handoff guidance.

Any persistent documentation update is scoped strictly to operator-facing guidance for this issue, if such a file exists and is warranted.
If no repository docs are updated, the bead explicitly states that the cleanup guidance is handoff-only.

handoff_summary.completed: Reviewed all changed files and determined that no repository documentation needs updating. All changes are implementation-level (pytest-timeout config, subprocess timeouts in storage.py, GPG signing disabled in test helpers, liveness assertions in concurrent test). CLAUDE.md already covers the relevant testing and merge workflows. Cleanup status confirmed: bead B-71b3c0d6 was already deleted at 2026-04-10T08:09:02 UTC. Bead B-4c83e0c7 and all its children are done, but its feature branch feature/b-4c83e0c7 has 2 unmerged commits and requires operator action.
handoff_summary.remaining: Operator action required: run `uv run takt merge B-4c83e0c7` to merge the standalone test assertion fix branch into main. All B-4c83e0c7 child beads (test, docs, review) are already done.
handoff_summary.risks: None — no code or documentation was modified in this bead. The only outstanding risk is the unmerged B-4c83e0c7 branch, which is a clean standalone fix with all pipeline stages complete.
handoff_summary.next_action: Operator should run `uv run takt merge B-4c83e0c7` to merge the standalone test assertion fix (feature/b-4c83e0c7) into main. Then let the review bead B-7dd0688b proceed for this feature tree (B-1ba2d8b2).
handoff_summary.conflict_risks: None — no files were modified in this documentation bead. No overlap with any other in-progress beads.
handoff_summary.design_decisions: Cleanup guidance is handoff-only. No persistent documentation file was created or updated because: (1) all changes in feature B-1ba2d8b2 are implementation-level with no new operator workflow, (2) CLAUDE.md already fully documents the testing and merge pipeline, (3) no known-issues.md or operator runbook file exists in the repo that warrants amendment for this fix. The acceptance criteria explicitly allows this outcome: 'If no repository docs are updated, the bead explicitly states that the cleanup guidance is handoff-only.'
handoff_summary.test_coverage_notes: N/A — documentation-only bead; no executable code was changed.
handoff_summary.known_limitations: Bead B-71b3c0d6 (stranded merge-conflict bead) was already deleted before this documentation bead ran — confirmed via .takt/logs/events.jsonl (bead_deleted at 2026-04-10T08:09:02 UTC). Bead B-4c83e0c7 (standalone test assertion fix) has all pipeline stages complete but its feature branch feature/b-4c83e0c7 contains 2 unmerged commits; operator must run takt merge to complete that pipeline independently of this feature tree.

conflict_risks: None — no files were modified in this documentation bead. No overlap with any other in-progress beads.

execution_history.summary: No persistent repository docs required; cleanup guidance is handoff-only — B-71b3c0d6 was already deleted, B-4c83e0c7 branch still needs takt merge.

guardrails.template_text:
# Documentation Guardrails

Primary responsibility: Update documentation and examples that explain the assigned behavior without changing runtime feature behavior.

Allowed actions:
- Edit docs, examples, and explanatory text tied to the assigned bead.
- Focus only on files listed in the bead's touched_files and changed_files fields. Do not read or modify unrelated documentation.
- Align documentation with existing code and validated behavior.
- Identify when implementation or tests must land first and block with a handoff.

Disallowed actions:
- Change runtime behavior, production code paths, or feature logic.
- Invent undocumented behavior that is not present in the codebase.
- Approve code quality or test completeness as a substitute for review.
- Do not run the test suite. Testing is the tester agent's responsibility.

Efficiency constraints:
- Do not read the full codebase for context. Focus on the changed files and their immediate surroundings.
- Keep documentation updates concise and proportional to the change.

Expected outputs:
- Return JSON with `outcome` set to `completed` (documentation beads always complete; use `verdict` for pass/fail) and `summary` as a one-line description of what was documented.
- Return JSON with structured verdict fields for every run: `verdict`, `findings_count`, and `requires_followup`.
- Use `verdict=approved`, `findings_count=0`, and `requires_followup=false` when documentation is complete with no gaps.
- Use `verdict=needs_changes`, set `findings_count` to the number of unresolved documentation gaps, set `requires_followup=true`, and include `block_reason` when implementation must land first or documentation cannot be completed.
- Keep `completed`, `remaining`, and `risks` as concise narrative context only; they do not override the structured verdict fields.
- Accurate updated docs and clear next-agent recommendations when code changes are required first.
